title: Converge release metadata into one canonical truth
outOfScope: Changing release publication or tag creation workflow
Changing evidence digest semantics beyond consistency validation
Publishing a release or changing release.json and next-release.json schema

sources.reason: Canonical release state currently conflicts with legacy metadata.
Published release metadata used to validate previousRelease.
Legacy candidate metadata used to validate candidate identity.
Existing release metadata validation and release claim semantics.
Existing canonical state and legacy metadata test coverage.
Current release state and metadata documentation.
Japanese release state and metadata documentation.
Work Item 1 acceptance and dependency boundary.
Reduce redundant formatting lines so the active Work Item status remains within the existing complexity budget; preserve all 17 items and requirements.

riskAssessment.reason: The change modifies release-state validation and canonical metadata while leaving publication behavior unchanged.

executionDecision.reason: The user authorized serial execution of Work Item 1 and the plan defines the release truth boundary.

preReviewWarnings: Verify published and candidate identity, previousRelease, state uniqueness, digest references, and no stale v0.5.35-only claim remains.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: release-state.json is the only canonical release state record for state, candidate tag, sourceCommit, and previousRelease.

check-release-state-consistency validates release-state.json against release.json and next-release.json and rejects conflicting identities, duplicate states, invalid references, and non-matching digests.
The current repository metadata passes the new consistency check without changing publication or tag creation behavior.
English and Japanese distribution documentation describe the canonical state and legacy metadata relationship accurately.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: release-state.json, release.json, and next-release.json currently express different published and candidate facts, so the repository has multiple competing release truths.
constraints: Preserve the public release contract consumed by installers.
Do not publish, move, or delete tags.
Keep digest placeholder semantics for the later Evidence Digest Work Item.
intent.rationale: A deterministic consistency checker and a corrected canonical state remove ambiguity before later source-commit and evidence-digest work.

rawUserRequest: 执行评审整改计划工单 1：收敛 One Release Truth，继续循环执行直到全部工单完成。

declaredIntent.summary: Make release-state.json the canonical release truth and validate legacy metadata consistency.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Published and candidate tags agree with canonical state
previousRelease equals the published release tag
Conflicting candidate, duplicate state, invalid digest reference, and skipped candidate are rejected
Release publication workflow behavior remains unchanged

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: The user authorized serial remediation including Makefile and CI entrypoint changes.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
